behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI categorization. MOCK-MISS: a unit test of categorizer logic can pass while real `git` output format/flags cause rename events to be emitted as add+delete. REAL-INFRA: behavior depends on subprocess `git` against an actual repo state. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text is a Python git CLI report with no AI/ML step.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo, commit a file, rename it with `git mv`, run the CLI as a subprocess, parse stdout report, assert rename count increments and added/deleted counts do not include that rename.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Failure is typically encoding/subprocess-output handling on real binary diffs. MOCK-HIDE: mocked text diff output can hide decode/crash issues triggered by real git binary diff behavior. REAL-INFRA: requires real filesystem bytes and real `git diff` subprocess behavior. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected; no model call in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo with a committed binary file and a changed binary file, run CLI via subprocess, assert exit code is success, no traceback on stderr, and binary file is included in report counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real unborn-HEAD git state and command exit behavior. Integration fakes can miss exact stderr/exit-code semantics from `git` in no-commit repos. INTEGRATION-BRAKE (a): yes, needs real subprocess/repo state, so not Integration. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior is purely git/CLI state handling.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run CLI subprocess, assert success exit code and structured empty report on stdout.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against actual git history semantics across commits/status categories. MOCK-MISS: canned fixtures can validate arithmetic but miss divergences from real git history interpretation. REAL-INFRA: requires real repo commits and real `git` subprocess queries. REJECTION-GATE: higher levels rejected because behavior text contains no AI/ML dependence or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a temp repo with a scripted history containing add/delete/rename/modify events, run CLI subprocess over that repo, and assert each reported category count matches expected counts derived from the known commit script.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real git index vs working-tree semantics. MOCK-HIDE: mocks can force equivalence and miss true differences in `git` plumbing outputs for staged/unstaged states. INTEGRATION-BRAKE (a): yes, real subprocess + real index/worktree state required. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected; no model decisions involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent changes once as unstaged and once staged, run CLI subprocess for each mode, normalize report ordering, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured output.
